title: alchemilla vulgaris subsp. minor
intro: Alchemilla vulgaris subsp. minor, also known as the small lady's mantle, is a subspecies of the lady's mantle plant, which is a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as soothing digestive issues, reducing menstrual cramps, and supporting wound healing. Alchemilla vulgaris subsp. minor can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly used to harness its medicinal properties.
